notes: Seeded recall corpus for the ship-gate precision fixture (spec 2085 slice 12). Each row is one live memory in namespace ant_worker on a distinct programming topic with its own vocabulary; each query names the row it should recall at rank one. Distinct domains keep each row the unique best lexical match, so BM25 alone clears the FTS-only floor and hybrid clears the higher bar. Edit rows and queries together; the fixture asserts at least fifty query-and-expected-row pairs.

rows.label: http client retries with jitter
rows.body: Wrap the http client in an exponential backoff with full jitter, cap the retries at five attempts, and only retry idempotent GET and PUT requests.
queries: exponential backoff full jitter for http retries
how many retry attempts before giving up on a request

rows.label: postgres partial index on active rows
rows.body: Add a partial btree index on the orders table filtered to status active, so the planner skips archived rows and the index stays small.
queries: partial btree index on active orders in postgres
keep the index small by filtering archived rows out

rows.label: goroutine leak from an unbuffered channel
rows.body: A goroutine blocked on send to an unbuffered channel nobody reads leaks forever; give the channel a buffer or add a context cancel to unblock it.
queries: goroutine leaks blocked sending on an unbuffered channel
unblock a stuck goroutine with a context cancel

rows.label: mutex lock ordering avoids deadlock
rows.body: Always acquire the account mutex before the ledger mutex; the reversed order between two transfers is the classic deadlock we hit last quarter.
queries: always lock the account mutex before the ledger mutex
deadlock from two transfers taking locks in reversed order

rows.label: compile the regex once at package init
rows.body: Compile the email regexp a single time with MustCompile at package init; compiling it per call showed up hot in the cpu profile.
queries: compile the email regexp once at package init
regexp compiled per call was hot in the cpu profile

rows.label: tls certificate renewal cron
rows.body: The lets encrypt certificate renews on a cron thirty days before expiry; a failed renewal pages oncall because the handshake starts rejecting clients.
queries: renew the lets encrypt tls certificate before expiry
failed certificate renewal makes the handshake reject clients

rows.label: base64 url encoding for tokens
rows.body: Encode the session token with base64 url encoding and no padding so it survives a query string; standard base64 breaks on the plus and slash bytes.
queries: base64 url encoding no padding for a session token
standard base64 plus and slash break in a query string

rows.label: validate utf8 before storing
rows.body: Reject a request body that is not valid utf8 at the edge; a lone surrogate half slipped into the database and corrupted the json export.
queries: reject invalid utf8 request bodies at the edge
a lone surrogate half corrupted the json export

rows.label: semaphore caps concurrent uploads
rows.body: A weighted semaphore caps concurrent s3 uploads at ten so a burst of jobs does not exhaust the file descriptors on the box.
queries: weighted semaphore caps concurrent s3 uploads
a burst of jobs exhausted the file descriptors

rows.label: lru cache eviction by size
rows.body: The lru cache evicts by total byte size not entry count, because a few large thumbnails were pinning small entries out of memory.
queries: lru cache evicts by total byte size not entry count
large thumbnails pinned small cache entries out

rows.label: binary search off by one on the high bound
rows.body: The binary search bug was an inclusive high bound; set high to len minus one and loop while low is less than or equal to high.
queries: binary search off by one on the inclusive high bound
set high to len minus one in the search loop

rows.label: parse timestamps as utc not local
rows.body: Parse incoming timestamps as utc explicitly; the server defaulted to local time and shifted every event by the daylight saving offset.
queries: parse incoming timestamps as utc not local time
daylight saving offset shifted every event

rows.label: round money with decimal not float
rows.body: Represent money as integer cents or a decimal type; summing float dollars drifted the invoice total by a penny over thousands of lines.
queries: represent money as integer cents not float dollars
summing floats drifted the invoice total by a penny

rows.label: csv writer quotes embedded commas
rows.body: The csv writer must quote a field with an embedded comma or newline; a customer address with a comma split into two columns downstream.
queries: csv writer quotes a field with an embedded comma
an address comma split into two columns downstream

rows.label: stream gzip instead of buffering
rows.body: Stream the gzip response through an io pipe rather than buffering the whole payload; the full buffer spiked memory on large report downloads.
queries: stream gzip through an io pipe not a full buffer
buffering the whole gzip payload spiked memory

rows.label: uuid v7 for time sortable ids
rows.body: Switch primary keys to uuid version seven so new rows sort by creation time and the btree index stops fragmenting on random v4 inserts.
queries: uuid version seven for time sortable primary keys
random v4 inserts fragmented the btree index

rows.label: cron expression runs in the wrong timezone
rows.body: The nightly cron ran an hour early after the deploy because the scheduler used the container utc clock instead of the configured business timezone.
queries: cron ran an hour early on the container utc clock
scheduler ignored the configured business timezone

rows.label: connection pool max sized to the database
rows.body: Size the database connection pool to the postgres max connections divided by the replica count, or a rolling deploy exhausts the server slots.
queries: size the connection pool to postgres max connections
a rolling deploy exhausted the database server slots

rows.label: prepared statement stops sql injection
rows.body: Bind user input through a prepared statement placeholder; the reported sql injection came from string concatenating a search term into the query.
queries: bind user input with a prepared statement placeholder
sql injection from concatenating a search term

rows.label: wal checkpoint under write load
rows.body: Force a sqlite wal checkpoint on a timer under heavy write load, or the wal file grows unbounded and swallows the disk before the passive checkpoint fires.
queries: force a sqlite wal checkpoint on a timer under write load
the wal file grew unbounded and filled the disk

rows.label: bloom filter skips missing key lookups
rows.body: A bloom filter in front of the disk store skips a lookup for a key that certainly does not exist, cutting the read amplification on cache misses.
queries: bloom filter skips lookups for keys that do not exist
cut read amplification on cache misses

rows.label: merkle tree verifies a synced range
rows.body: Compare merkle tree root hashes between replicas to find a diverged key range without shipping every row across the wire.
queries: merkle tree root hashes find a diverged key range
verify a synced range without shipping every row

rows.label: protobuf varint decoding is little endian groups
rows.body: A protobuf varint packs seven bits per byte with the high bit as a continuation flag; a truncated stream leaves the continuation set and never terminates.
queries: protobuf varint seven bits per byte continuation flag
a truncated varint stream never terminates

rows.label: websocket ping keeps the proxy open
rows.body: Send a websocket ping every thirty seconds or the load balancer idle timeout closes the connection and the client sees a silent disconnect.
queries: websocket ping every thirty seconds beats the idle timeout
the load balancer closed the idle websocket connection

rows.label: dns resolution caching in the resolver
rows.body: The go resolver does not cache dns by default, so a hot outbound host hammered the dns server; add a short ttl cache in front of the dialer.
queries: the go resolver does not cache dns by default
a hot host hammered the dns server add a ttl cache

rows.label: arena allocator cuts gc pressure
rows.body: Batch the per request scratch buffers into an arena allocator reset at the end of the request so the garbage collector stops scanning short lived junk.
queries: arena allocator reset per request cuts gc pressure
stop the collector scanning short lived scratch buffers

rows.label: signal handling drains before exit
rows.body: Trap sigterm and drain the in flight requests with a grace period before exit, so a kubernetes rollout does not drop live connections mid response.
queries: trap sigterm and drain in flight requests before exit
a kubernetes rollout dropped live connections mid response

rows.label: rate limiter uses a token bucket
rows.body: The public api rate limiter is a token bucket refilled at a steady rate with a burst allowance, keyed per api key in a redis sorted set.
queries: token bucket rate limiter with a burst allowance
rate limit keyed per api key in a redis sorted set

rows.label: idempotency key dedupes double submits
rows.body: Require an idempotency key on the payment endpoint and store the first response, so a client retry after a timeout does not charge the card twice.
queries: idempotency key on the payment endpoint dedupes retries
a timeout retry charged the card twice without a key

rows.label: pagination by keyset not offset
rows.body: Paginate large result sets by keyset on the sorted id rather than sql offset, because a deep offset scans and discards every earlier row.
queries: keyset pagination on the sorted id not sql offset
a deep offset scanned and discarded every earlier row
